feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration Error: HTTP header is larger than 8192 bytes.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When processing a large payload in Cloud Integration with a S/4HANA Cloud target system using HTTP Adapter, I got the following error:

io.netty.handler.codec.TooLongFrameException: HTTP header is larger than 8192 bytes

 

I followed SAP Note 3146809(https://me.sap.com/notes/3146809/E) and tried the resolution(set "Cookie|x-csrf-token as Request Headers) , but the error still remained.

(P.s. I have also checked SAP Note 3537811 (https://me.sap.com/notes/3537811/E) but it does not really help because it merely asks me to reduce the size of HTTP header getting returned without letting me know how)

Does anyone know how to fix this?

===================================

As your reference, the following screenshots show the setting in my integration flow, which relate to http headers.

entry.title: CPI - Adapter RabbitMQ consumer Message - Wrong converstion to base64
entry.content:
I am using SAP CPI with a Groovy script, and when I post this message to the queue, it appears as Base64. However, in the logs of my middleware (SAP CPI), it is shown as a regular message.

When I post it to an online webhook, it also arrives normally as JSON.

Only in Adapter(AMQP)RabbitMQ does it arrive in Base64. The version is 4.0, and AMQP 1.0 is being used.

 

Message encode64 post in rabbit

eyJwYXR0ZXJuIjoidGVzdC5ldnQiLCJkYXRhIjp7IklEIjoiNjYzIiwiS2V5U0FQIjoiMDAwMSIsIlN0YXR1cyI6IlMiLCJNZW5zYWdlU0FQIjoiQ2xpZW50ZSAwMDAxIGNyZWF0ZWQgd2l0aCBzdWNjZXNzLiJ9fQ== 

Coding Groovy:

 

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

Message processData(Message message) { 
def fixedBody = '{"pattern":"test.evt","data":{"ID":"663","KeySAP":"0001","Status":"S","MensageSAP":"Cliente 0001 created with success."}}'

message.setBody(fixedBody)


message.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/plain")
message.setHeader("CamelHttpCharacterEncoding", "UTF-8")

return message 
}

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: Transporting Script Collection
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

     I have a question regarding usage of Script Collections in SAP Integration Suite.

Context: In my case, script collection contains the common scripts being utilized by multiple iFlows. 

Issue: There are few Groovy scripts in the script collection which are complete and tested. However, there are few scripts which are currently being worked upon or not yet fully tested. Now I want to transport the scripts to QA/Prod systems which are complete. However, the transport option is only available at the Script Collection Object Level but not at individual script level which means transporting the whole script collection will transport the incomplete/untested scripts as well. How can this be avoided?

#SAPIntegrationSuite, #SAPiFlow #SAPCPI

Thanks and Regards,

    Abhijeet

entry.title: How to handle constantly changing host key of SFTP server
entry.content:
Hello, 
I've a requirement of maintaining the known host file of an SFTP server, but each time I'm trying to obtain the host key fingerprint in the connectivity test in BTP monitoring, I'm getting a different host key every time and it is proving to be hard to maintain it in the known host file. 

when I tried to communicate to the owner of the SFTP server, they said they have set it in a way that it'll change dynamically each time we try to obtain the key. Which is why I'm getting an error while connecting to the server afterwards.

any ideas about how to go about this?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Push all individual records in a single mail
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am getting response from the receiver system as individual records(one by one), Now I just want to send all the records at a time in a single mail.

I know we can achieve this using gather option, but I want to achieve this using data store operation.

I just tried with write and select data store operations but select operation is triggering multiple times whenever the record is coming but I want it to trigger after getting all records.

Could anyone help me on this?

Thanks,

Pavan Koppula

entry.title: XML Namespace Error with XPATH condition
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have below as the content which needs to be routed based on the field 'Message' value. 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<error xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata">
  <code>06/019</code>
  <message xml:lang="en">Document  does not exist</message>
  <innererror>
    <application>
      <component_id/>
      <service_namespace>/SAP/</service_namespace>
      <service_id>TEST</service_id>
      <service_version>0001</service_version>
    </application>
    <transactionid>d828d56baed54a718432432e7cd27533</transactionid>
    <timestamp/>
    <Error_Resolution>
      <SAP_Transaction/>
      <SAP_Note>See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)</SAP_Note>
    </Error_Resolution>
    <errordetails>
      <errordetail>
        <ContentID/>
        <code>06/033</code>
        <message>Document  does not exist</message>
        <propertyref/>
        <severity>error</severity>
        <target/>
        <transition>false</transition>
      </errordetail>
    </errordetails>
  </innererror>
</error>

 

Tried the condition = //innererror/errordetails/errordetail[contains(message, 'Document')]

Somehow, it is not working due to the namespace. I've also added the namespace in the runtime configuration as xmlns=http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata Still getting invalid namespace declared error. 

Appreciate your valuable inputs and support. 

Thanks,

Barath Vivekanandan

entry.title: SAP ECC to BTP Integration Suite Proxy connection
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have the following situation where sender SAP ECC system would like to send XML proxy message to BTP Integration Suite.. whereas we also have SAP PO in our landscape.. SAP ECC ABAP developer would like to get our SAP PO ESR objects reflected on their SAP ECC system.. But, in runtime the messages should flow via BTP Integration Suite.. How to acheive this , should we ask BASIS guys to create two G type destinations on SAP ECC like mentioned below:

1.  "G" type destination(SAP_PROXY_ESR) to connect to SAP PO ESR. With the help of this destination, ESR objects should be visible in SAP ECC (SPROXY).

2. Another "G" type destination to route the messages to BTP Integration Suite.

Could you please guide me to acheive the above requirement?

Please let me know if something important is missed.

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: CPI Adapter RabbitMQ cannot post header content to MQ
entry.content:
Hi,Expert:

  I tested a simple integration flow and added custom header [url] information to the content modifier. When I tested and found that the header information would not be passed to MQ, this is why ?

Thanks，

Sean

1.My integration flow ：

2.Set Header：

3.RabbitMQ Set

4.Test ：My Custome Headr [url] not send to mq

entry.title: No run steps visible in sap cpi trace
entry.content:
Hi ,

No run steps visible in sap cpi trace.

what could be the reason plz suggest.

entry.title: Messages struck in event mesh
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

I'm not able to understand the reason for messages getting struck in the event mesh and why is CPI not able to poll the messages successfully. This happens every now and then. below is the configuration of AMQP adapter.

How to fix this?

entry.title: Webservice call error from S4HANA/ECC to BTP CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Our requirement is to send data from ECC/S4HANA to SAP Integration Suite(CPI) where we generated URL after creating SOAP sender channel on CPI.

On SAP backend they are trying to generate Webservices in SOAMANAGER and when I try to ping the webservice after configuring CPI URL. Later, when I "Ping Webservice" from then it throws the following error(shown in screenshots).

Should we generate SSL certificates on CPI iflow level and then share with S4HANA/ECC Basis guys? Also should we ask Certificates from SAP backend to import it to CPI iflow level? 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Integration Suite Trace Level is not working
entry.content:
After the most recent update, I've noticed there are strange behaviours coming from activating trace in IS, in particular, I can't seem to activate Trace level logs in the following iFlow. It remains as Log Level: Info for any runs that I do:

even if the current configuration should be on Trace: 

 I have tried to redeploy the iFlow, put the log level in debug, error and info then back to Trace.

 

Nothing changes.

 

Is anyone having the same issue? Any input or help would be appreciated!

entry.title: Not able to activate Trading partner Agreement with MIG using custom message
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I created a MIG with a custom X12 structure. When I tried to use this in trading partner agreement and activate it , I am getting the below error.

Error was : Failed to publish Message Implementation Guideline with id 4b6dd29d12024347b1d9b875c1c2b345 to Partner Directory. Error was : Error executing [PUSH_TO_PD] on service [SAP Integration Advisor]. Response was [Code: 500, Reason : Internal Server Error : {"errorCode":"FAILED_TO_PUSH_ARTIFACT_TO_CPI","errorMessage":"Cannot invoke \"com.sap.it.spc.smarti.ica.exporter.util.ArtifactHolder.getContent()\" because \"msgXsd\" is null"}]

Can anyone please help ?

entry.title: Subscribe Path in Autoflow
entry.content:
I create Autoflow in SAP Sales Cloud V2 for Sales Order in order to create data from sales cloud v2 to s4hana public cloud system via Integration Suite.

I would like to know which path should be entered for Subscribe Path.

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite OData V4 receiving format JSON
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to create Sales Order in SAP Public Cloud with help of Sales Order Integration, SAP_COM_0109. I am getting error "Unexpected character '{' (code 123) in prolog at [row,col {unknown-source}]: [1,2]". In note 3266527 stays: "Use XML". Why? When I run Postmen with same endpoint and JSON body I am able to create Sales Order. Why in iFlow I must convert it to XML or there is some way to enable JSON as accepted format?

 

Thank you

entry.title: SAP Datasphere and Open connectors what is supposed to be "access method BULK" ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a very slow Data Flow in Datasphere, due to the fact that a source is from an Open Connector connection.

The Dataflow is very slow, i don't know if it is due to the connection between the source and SAP Integration suite, or between SAP integration suite and dataflows, or inside the dataflow itself.

What is my idea based on observations?

With open connectors the datatypes set for column of type STRING is "STRING(5000)"  (for every STRING columns in the source!!!).

So a possible problem that I found is that when I raise batch size the dataflow => it runs into an error and say "out of memory". This makes me think that dataflow for some reason preallocate memory based on datatypes of the columns. Many columns like "IDs" that may have up to 10 character are treated as STRING(5000).

And this datatype can not be changed in the source property inside Datasphere.

But this is all hypothetical based on observations.

Another observation is that the first rows are written with high speed, the last rows are incredibly slow. this makes me think that the extraction made from the open connector do something like this:

 

read the first 1000 rows and process them,

read the first 2000 rows and process the last 1000.

..

read the first 100000 rows and process the last 1000.

I believe the rows are fetched multiple times from a source and only the last are processed, and this due to the times it takes to download the content (around 200K rows x 100 columns (many of which have a STRING datatype)).

 

A thing that I notice is that we have options inside dataflows that are specific for Open Connectors.

and that are not documented in me.sap.com nor in help.sap.com.

 

So my question is what exactly do the option "Access Method" under the Advanced panel of a source of a dataflow in case of Open connectors?

EDIT: Another problem I found is that with this option ON, the columns with datatype timestamp from the source will generate an error in dataflow execution. => this BULK option is not usable...

Best Regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Login to Integration suite, but the Menu didn't show up.
entry.content:
I install Integration suite, but the Menu didn't show up.

This concerning the Task 2 of "Learning Journey" on Integration suite

Please could you help me ?

In the exercie , i suppose to have Menu : see screenshot for exercise

Thanks

entry.title: How to Handle Missing Source Values and Default to Empty String in SAP CPI Mapping?
entry.content:
I’m working on an integration in SAP CPI where I need to handle missing or null values in the mapping. Specifically, I want to ensure that when there is no value for a specific source field, the target JSON payload includes "value": "". (see attached picture for current mapping). 

Original Problem:

We are encountering the following error in the flow:

"Error occurred during custom_attributes processing. The responsibilities_employee attribute code doesn't have a value set. Enter the value and try again."

The current payload looks like this:
{
"attribute_code": "responsibilities_employee"
} 

The requirement is that when there is no value for the attribute (e.g., responsibilities_employee), we need the output to include:

{
"attribute_code": "responsibilities_employee",
"value": ""
} 

I have tried the following:

  1. I used the exists function to check if the input field exists.
  2. I connected it to an ifS function to return either the source value (if it exists) or "" (empty string) if it does not.
  3. I passed this output to a concat function to join the values (GivenName and FamilyName), with a space as a delimiter.

Here’s the simplified flow I’ve been building:

  • GivenName → ifS → concat
  • FamilyName → ifS → concat
  • concat → value

However, it’s still not working as expected, and when the source value is missing, the value tag is missing entirely in the output payload instead of defaulting to "".

How can adjust the mapping in order for it to work?

For the sake of clarity. When the field is populated it looks like this:
{
"attribute_code": "responsibilities_employee",
"value": "John Doe"
},

entry.title: How to add attachement to Message?
entry.content:
Hi all, we currently have an interface between SAP and Salesforce on BTP. When we have wrong or missing data, the user in salesforce gets the same error message as its shown in CPI Monitoring Status:

Error Details
com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException: An internal server error occured: HTTP operation failed invoking https://XXXondemand.com/odata/SSCO/SF_BPARTNER_DATA_SRV/HeaderSet with statusCode: 500. The MPL ID for the failed message is : XXX
 
In CPI we have 3 attachments to each error, two headers, one body: 
HTTP_Receiver_Adapter_Response_Body
 
I want to extract the text of the body (the message we get from SAP) and add it to the status (which is also the notification in salesforce). Here is a text what data is wrong, so the user could correct it directly. We have two groovy scripts. Could anyone please help me to do so? I tried with GPT but all we could do is: "An internal server error occured: No signature of method: com.sap.it.op.agent.mpl.factory.impl.MessageLogImpl.getAttachment() is applicable for argument types: (java.lang.String) values: [ErrorBody]."
Unfortunatly the extraction of the attachment didnt work.
 
I will paste the original codes, without any transformation.

 

//* Groovy Error Exception Handling

package com.scco.crm
 
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLog
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLogFactory
 
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
 
import java.util.concurrent.ThreadLocalRandom
import java.util.HashMap
 
import org.apache.camel.CamelException;
import org.apache.camel.component.ahc.AhcOperationFailedException
import org.apache.camel.component.cxf.CxfOperationException
 
/**
* Builds a general error response for the CRM-ERP integration APIs.
* 
* @Param message the iFlow's Message instance.
* @return the possibly modified iFlow's Message instance.
*/
def Message processData(Message message) {
	MessageLog messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
 
	String body = message.getBody(String.class)
	messageLog.addAttachmentAsString('LastResponseBody', body != null ? body : '(null)', "text/plain")
	String errorBody = null // the ordinary body may not be the error body, we derive the error body later
	Map<String,String> response = new HashMap<>()
	Map<String,String> fields = new HashMap<>()
	int internalStatusCode = 0
	Map<String, Object> map = message.getProperties();
	def ex = map.get("CamelExceptionCaught");
	if (ex instanceof Exception) {
		String exClassName = ex.getClass().getName()
		messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('CamelExceptionCaught', exClassName)
		response.put("cause", exClassName)
		if (isSpecificCamelException(ex, messageLog)) {
			if (isNoneEmpty(ex.getResponseBody())) {
				errorBody = ex.getResponseBody()
				fields.put("causeErrorBody", errorBody)
			}
			internalStatusCode = ex.getStatusCode()
     		fields.put("causeStatus", "" + ex.getStatusCode() + " (" + ex.getStatusText() + ')')
		}
		if (internalStatusCode == 503) {
			response.put("message", "A necessary downstream service is - possibly temporarily - unavailable. After a while try the request again! - Original error: " + ex.getResponseBody())
			response.put("errorCode", "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE")
		} else {
			response.put("message", ex.getResponseBody())
			response.put("errorCode", "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR") // we can not identify a specific error
		}
	} else {
		response.put("message", "An unspecified error occurred. Please contact your administrator.")
        response.put("errorCode", "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR") // here we can not identify a specific error
	}
	response.put("fields",fields);
	if (!isNoneEmpty(errorBody)) {
		errorBody = '(empty)'
	}
	messageLog.addAttachmentAsString('ErrorBody', errorBody, "text/plain")
	messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('Status_business', 'failed')
	message.setBody(new JsonBuilder(response).toString())
	message.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/json")
	message.setHeader("CamelHttpResponseCode", 500)
	return message;
}
 
/**
* Checks if the provided object is a {@link CamelException} and has the specific fields 'responseBody', 'statusCode' and statusText'.
* @Param ex the object to test.
* @return TRUE if if the provided object is a {@link CamelException} and has the specific fields described above.
*/ 
def boolean isSpecificCamelException(Object ex, MessageLog messageLog) {
	if (ex instanceof CamelException) {
		Class exClass = ex.getClass()
		try {
			// following fields has to be exist:
			exClass.getMethod('getResponseBody', null)
			exClass.getMethod('getStatusCode', null)
			exClass.getMethod('getStatusText', null)
		} catch (NoSuchMethodException|SecurityException e) {
			messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('NoSuchMethod', e.toString())
			return false;
		}
		return true;
	}
	messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty('NoCamelException', ex.getClass().getName())
	return false;
}
 
def boolean isNoneEmpty(Object o) {
	if (o instanceof CharSequence) {
		return o != null && o.length() > 0
	} else if (o instanceof Collection) {
		return o != null && !o.isEmpty()
	}
	return o != null;
}
 ​

 

 

 

//*groovy skript SAP Message
package com.scco.crm.update_erp_debtor
 
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLog
import com.sap.it.api.msglog.MessageLogFactory
 
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
 
import java.util.concurrent.ThreadLocalRandom
import java.util.HashMap
 
def Message processData(Message message) {
	def MessageLog messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
	String bodyAsString = message.getBody(String.class)
 
	messageLog.addAttachmentAsString('ERP-Update_ResponseBody', bodyAsString, "text/plain")
 
	return message;
}
